conversations.say: Maren, I accept your terms. We must protect evaluator anonymity and never release workflow evidence without prior worker consent.
conversations.offer: Agreement on strict worker protection boundaries and mutual support.
conversations.request: Joint public stance against turning anonymous contributors into disposable mystery currency.

conversations.say: Jules, if you seek succession, you must explicitly bind yourself to protecting every contributor's name and labor rights in writing.
conversations.offer: Conditional consideration of support if governance structures prioritize worker safety.
conversations.request: A clear commitment to protecting contributors from unearned exposure.

conversations.say: Eli, your technical logs cannot establish who authorized or designed activity during the disputed interval. We must ensure your analysis doesn't expose unnamed contributors.
conversations.offer: Shared contributor-schedule comparison boundaries without disclosing personal names.
conversations.request: Clear acknowledgment of what the gaps in the logs cannot prove.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R was built by working hands, shared drops, and invisible labor. Any reckoning must protect the contributors, not sacrifice them to personal legend or algorithmic speculation.

commitments.promise: Protect evaluator anonymity and never release workflow evidence without prior worker consent.

goal_progress: Accepted Maren's shared disclosure boundary commitment to protect anonymous contributors.
Pressed Jules for concrete worker protections under any succession model.
Engaged Eli to establish boundaries around contributor-schedule comparisons without naming individuals.
reasoning_summary: Secured a formal commitment with Maren protecting anonymous contributors, challenged Jules on succession accountability, and coordinated with Eli to ensure technical logs do not misinterpret the disputed activity interval as proof of individual authorship.
